title: ED violence/aggression incident documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.
red_flags.rationale: The exact NICE risk and de-escalation section supports recording associated context, relevant negatives, patient concerns and an objective clinician risk context, including triggers and previously effective de-escalation, without asserting dangerousness or selecting an intervention.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.14 and 1.3.12–1.3.20 — risk assessment and de-escalation

examination.text: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.
examination.rationale: The exact safety-monitoring section supports measured vital signs, manual focused examination, severity, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only if an intervention or injury was actually assessed; medicine and restraint instructions are excluded.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings

treatment_and_management.text: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact post-incident section supports recording clinician-decided disposition, reviewed reports, documentation plan, completed discussion and arranged handover or review after an incident; it does not authorise an intervention.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.53–1.4.63 — post-incident debrief, documentation and review

source_limitations: Exact incident chronology, location, people involved, antecedents, verbal and physical behaviour, injuries, capacity, intoxication, restraint details, medicines, security or police involvement, witnesses and local incident identifiers remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
